jobs.title: Director, Toxicology — Nonclinical Strategy & Safety Lead
jobs.description:
Evommune, Inc. in Palo Alto seeks a Director of Toxicology to lead nonclinical safety strategy across development programs and to oversee IND-enabling toxicology studies.The role requires strong scientific judgment, CRO oversight, and collaboration with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced biotech setting. The successful candidate will design, interpret, and report toxicology studies, align with regulatory expectations, and contribute to regulatory submissions and health authority#J-18808-Ljbffr
Responsibilities
The role requires strong scientific judgment, CRO oversight, and collaboration with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced biotech setting
The successful candidate will design, interpret, and report toxicology studies, align with regulatory expectations, and contribute to regulatory submissions and health authority

jobs.title: Computational Toxicologist Drug Discovery & Liver Safety
jobs.description:
Position: Computational Toxicologist for Drug Discovery & Liver Safety Axiom is building a computational toxicology platform and you will help establish the foundation for toxicity prediction using multimodal datasets and collaboration with ML researchers.You will analyze high-content imaging, transcriptomics, and proteomics from human hepatocytes and hepatic systems, develop robust features and QC, and bridge wet lab protocols with machine learning models to explain mechanisms and guide drug design.#J-18808-Ljbffr
Responsibilities
Axiom is building a computational toxicology platform and you will help establish the foundation for toxicity prediction using multimodal datasets and collaboration with ML researchers
You will analyze high-content imaging, transcriptomics, and proteomics from human hepatocytes and hepatic systems, develop robust features and QC, and bridge wet lab protocols with machine learning models to explain mechanisms and guide drug design

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
Name of Employer: GSI Environmental Inc.

Position Location: 2000 Powell Street, Suite 820, Emeryville, CA 94608.

Telecommuting is permitted, but applicants must live within a reasonable commuting distance.

Salary: $96,533.00 — $110,336.20 per year

Hours: Monday – Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm

Summary Of Duties

Research, acquire, compile, and manage data for environmental projects.Evaluate environmental project data and information to assess site condition.Coordinate and oversee field and laboratory programs.Perform computer modeling and data analysis to develop solutions to environmental problems.Prepare documentations and reports.Assist PTLs and Principals with preparation of proposals (scopes of work and budgets).Participate in environmental project team meetings.Develop professional network by participating in professional societies and conference attendance.

Qualifications

Education requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, or related field or foreign equivalent.

Experience requirements: 2 years of experience in the job offered or related role.

Special Skills or Other Requirements: 2 years of experience must include:

Landslide and debris flow simulation software, civil engineering, and geological hazard assessment.Hydrologic and hydraulic (H&H) modeling for flood mapping, groundwater modeling, and sediment transport analysis.Statistical analysis of large climate datasets, including trend analysis, Bayesian approach, and climate change analysis.Machine learning and deep learning models to analyze groundwater level and streamflow, spatial data, and satellite images.Developing and applying numerical models (Finite Difference/Finite Volume) for hydraulic and structural integrity analysis for dam safety and breach modeling.Unsupervised clustering and classification techniques for geospatial data, including soil types and geological mapping.Freshwater modeling.

Travel requirements: 5% domestic travel and 5% international travel.
Qualifications
Education requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, or related field or foreign equivalent
Experience requirements: 2 years of experience in the job offered or related role
Special Skills or Other Requirements: 2 years of experience must include:
Landslide and debris flow simulation software, civil engineering, and geological hazard assessment
Hydrologic and hydraulic (H&H) modeling for flood mapping, groundwater modeling, and sediment transport analysis
Statistical analysis of large climate datasets, including trend analysis, Bayesian approach, and climate change analysis
Machine learning and deep learning models to analyze groundwater level and streamflow, spatial data, and satellite images
Developing and applying numerical models (Finite Difference/Finite Volume) for hydraulic and structural integrity analysis for dam safety and breach modeling
Unsupervised clustering and classification techniques for geospatial data, including soil types and geological mapping
Freshwater modeling
Travel requirements: 5% domestic travel and 5% international travel
Benefits
Salary: $96,533.00 — $110,336.20 per year
Responsibilities
Hours: Monday – Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm
Research, acquire, compile, and manage data for environmental projects
Evaluate environmental project data and information to assess site condition
Coordinate and oversee field and laboratory programs
Perform computer modeling and data analysis to develop solutions to environmental problems
Prepare documentations and reports
Assist PTLs and Principals with preparation of proposals (scopes of work and budgets)
Participate in environmental project team meetings
Develop professional network by participating in professional societies and conference attendance

jobs.title: Nurse Practitioner PRN - Health Risk Assessments
jobs.description:
Hueman is actively hiring Nurse Practitioners to complete in‑home Health Risk Assessments (HRAs) for Medicare and Medicaid members in and around South San Francisco, CA.
This unique opportunity adapts to your lifestyle, giving you the freedom to achieve your professional goals on your terms, all while enjoying a schedule designed with your preferences in mind.
Whether it be for supplemental income or the desire for community connection, this position invites you to become an integral part of the thriving HRA landscape, fostering connections that go beyond the clinic.
Position Summary Qualified Nurse Practitioners interested in this position will have the opportunity to interact with members in a more intimate setting.
Unlike a typical clinic visit, this role allows NPs to connect with patients on a more personal and effective level.
Nurse Practitioners performing HRAs are not required to treat or prescribe in the home.
Nurse Practitioner Responsibilities Travel to and from patients' homes.
Perform comprehensive, non‑invasive physical assessments.
Review medical history and ensure accurate and timely documentation.
Conduct medical reconciliation to ensure patient safety and compliance.
Provide basic patient education on wellness and preventative healthcare measures.
Nurse Practitioner Qualifications and Experience Requirements Master's Degree or commensurate experience and satisfactory completion of NP license.
Active State NP license.
Active National board certification.
Current BLS certification.
12 months of experience as a paid advanced clinical provider is preferred but not required.
Benefits Competitive compensation and travel reimbursement.
Flexible schedule.
Comprehensive home assessment training.
Dedicated patient scheduling assistance.
Liability insurance options.
To learn more about this position, please use this link to schedule a call with a member of our recruitment team.
About Hueman:
 At Hueman, we're dedicated to guiding you toward fulfilling career opportunities.
We believe that happiness should be a fundamental part of every role in risk assessment.
Our opportunities provide flexibility, extra income, and integration into your local healthcare network.
If this resonates with your idea of career satisfaction, allow us to assist you in discovering your next healthcare position!
•
Qualifications
Nurse Practitioner Qualifications and Experience Requirements Master's Degree or commensurate experience and satisfactory completion of NP license
Active State NP license
Active National board certification
Current BLS certification
Benefits
This unique opportunity adapts to your lifestyle, giving you the freedom to achieve your professional goals on your terms, all while enjoying a schedule designed with your preferences in mind
Benefits Competitive compensation and travel reimbursement
Flexible schedule
Comprehensive home assessment training
Dedicated patient scheduling assistance
Responsibilities
Position Summary Qualified Nurse Practitioners interested in this position will have the opportunity to interact with members in a more intimate setting
Unlike a typical clinic visit, this role allows NPs to connect with patients on a more personal and effective level
Nurse Practitioners performing HRAs are not required to treat or prescribe in the home
Nurse Practitioner Responsibilities Travel to and from patients' homes
Perform comprehensive, non‑invasive physical assessments
Review medical history and ensure accurate and timely documentation
Conduct medical reconciliation to ensure patient safety and compliance
Provide basic patient education on wellness and preventative healthcare measures

jobs.title: On-site Environmental Compliance Leader
jobs.description:
Hims & Hers is seeking an Environmental Compliance Specialist to serve as the on‑site lead at our Menlo Park, CA facility. You will manage hazardous waste, air quality, wastewater, stormwater, and regulatory reporting with a high degree of independence.

You will report to senior environmental leadership and coordinate with regulators, vendors, and operations to ensure full compliance and continuous improvement in line with California requirements.
Responsibilities
You will manage hazardous waste, air quality, wastewater, stormwater, and regulatory reporting with a high degree of independence
You will report to senior environmental leadership and coordinate with regulators, vendors, and operations to ensure full compliance and continuous improvement in line with California requirements

jobs.title: Environmental Specialist
jobs.description:
SUMMARY 

Mortenson is currently seeking an Environmental Specialist with the ability to interact with a variety of staff at all levels in an ever-changing environment, remaining flexible, resourceful, proactive, and efficient with a high level of professionalism and confidentiality.  The Environmental Specialist will support Mortenson’s environmental management efforts for our Seattle and Portland commercial construction projects with potential to support additional commercial groups beyond the Pacific Northwest. This individual will be providing review of environmental documentation and advising on environmental matters in a manner consistent with company policies and procedures.

This individual can be based out of either our Portland, OR or Seattle, WA office.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Implement Mortenson’s environmental program for simple to moderately complex project sites and promulgate environmental compliance of construction activities.  Essential requirements:Develop site specific environmental plans and activitiesReview environmental submittals and attend preconstruction and preparatory meetingsImplement policies and procedures to ensure compliance with environmental regulationsDevelop and lead environmental orientation and training programsLead onsite best management practices for environmental managementMonitor implementation of environmental mitigation measures and conduct work site environmental inspectionsCollaborate with customers on regulatory (Federal, State, Provincial and local) reporting and documentation requirementsMaintain operational knowledge of environmental issues in construction, and other applicable standards, regulations, laws, and proceduresInterface with customer’s environmental personnel, environmental consultants, regulatory representatives, etc.Assist in the development company-wide Environmental Management supportive programs 

QUALIFICATIONS

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Geology, Environmental Management, Environmental Science, Biology, or related field Minimum of four years related experienceSignificant experience with two or more of the following areas: erosion prevention and sediment control, storm water pollution prevention plans, spill prevention controls and countermeasures, wetlands, endangered speciesStrong understanding of Federal, State, Provincial and local laws, policies and proceduresA well-organized and self-directed individual who is collaborative and a team playerAbility to interact with consultants and governmental agencies Ability to use PC, tablets and widely used software packages, e.g., spreadsheets (Excel), word processing (MS Word)Current driver’s licenseAble to travel up to 50%  

A few benefits offered include: 

(for Non-Craft & Non-Union Craft working 25+ hours / week)

Medical and prescription drug plans that includes no additional cost vision coverage Dental plan 401k retirement plan with a generous Mortenson match Paid time off, holidays, and other paid leaves Employer paid Life, AD&D, and disability insurance No-Cost mental health tool and concierge with extensive work-life resourcesTuition reimbursement Adoption Assistance Gym Membership Discount Program  

The base pay range for this role is $89,500 - $120,800. (Actual range is higher for the following office locations: Denver, CO and Chicago, IL – 5%, Seattle, WA, and Portland, OR – 10%).

Base pay is positioned within the range based on several factors including an individual’s knowledge, skills, and experience, with consideration given to internal equity.  This position is eligible for Mortenson’s incentive plan.

#LI-BS1

Please make note: 

Visa sponsorship is not offered for this position.Our postings are typically open a minimum of 5 days and an average of 44 days.  

ABOUT MORTENSON

As a top builder, developer, and EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction), our expertise spans markets like sports, renewable energy, data centers, healthcare, and more. We are builders at heart, working to ensure the built environment has a lasting positive impact. 

Let’s Redefine Possible®  

Equal Employment Opportunity

Your uniqueness brings new and creative perspectives to the team. Mortenson is committed to providing equal opportunities of employment (EOE) to all individuals, regardless of your race, religion, gender, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, marital status or any other legally protected category.

Other Items to Note

Mortenson reserves the right to hire any individual without legal or financial obligation on unwanted solicitations. No agency emails, calls, or solicitations are accepted without a valid agreement.Must be currently legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status, 0-1, TN, CPT, OPT, etc.). We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree or higher in Geology, Environmental Management, Environmental Science, Biology, or related field
Minimum of four years related experience
Significant experience with two or more of the following areas: erosion prevention and sediment control, storm water pollution prevention plans, spill prevention controls and countermeasures, wetlands, endangered species
Strong understanding of Federal, State, Provincial and local laws, policies and procedures
A well-organized and self-directed individual who is collaborative and a team player
Ability to interact with consultants and governmental agencies
Ability to use PC, tablets and widely used software packages, e.g., spreadsheets (Excel), word processing (MS Word)
Current driver’s license
Able to travel up to 50%
Must be currently legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status, 0-1, TN, CPT, OPT, etc.). We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time
Benefits
(for Non-Craft & Non-Union Craft working 25+ hours / week)
Medical and prescriptio

new_jobs.title: Computational Toxicologist Drug Discovery & Liver Safety
new_jobs.description:
Position: Computational Toxicologist for Drug Discovery & Liver Safety Axiom is building a computational toxicology platform and you will help establish the foundation for toxicity prediction using multimodal datasets and collaboration with ML researchers.You will analyze high-content imaging, transcriptomics, and proteomics from human hepatocytes and hepatic systems, develop robust features and QC, and bridge wet lab protocols with machine learning models to explain mechanisms and guide drug design.#J-18808-Ljbffr

Axiom is building a computational toxicology platform and you will help establish the foundation for toxicity prediction using multimodal datasets and collaboration with ML researchers
You will analyze high-content imaging, transcriptomics, and proteomics from human hepatocytes and hepatic systems, develop robust features and QC, and bridge wet lab protocols with machine learning models to explain mechanisms and guide drug design
